metadata.title:
MUST REPAIR


CAR TRACKS

COUNCIL AWAkEs TO WORM
OUT CONDITION OF PAVE
MENT ALONG w. w. P. LINES


ON MAIN BUSINESS STREETS.

pages.raw_text:
On recommendation Of Council
man brown, the board of public
works was directed last night to
enforce upon the street railways
stricter compliance with regulations
regarding conditions of tracks. The
Washington Water Power Co., in
particular, has disregarded it's
franchises and allowed its roadbed
in places to go to ruin SO that pub
lie safety is endangered. Along
Riverside av., the principal street,
the granite blocks bordering the
tracks are worn away making the
street rough and uneven, so that
rigs attempting to turn are some
times almost tipped over. The
same condition prevails on North
Monroe.


Councilman Brown said: "Such
conditions should not exist. With
the Interstate fair only few days
off these tracks should be put in
better order. There will be more
travel and consequently more diff
ficulties by reason of track condi-
tons"
